mnemonics.title: COMBINATIONAL vs SEQUENTIAL
mnemonics.content: C-S-L-M: Combinational = Same inputs give Same outputs (no Memory). Sequential = Same inputs can give Different outputs (has Memory).

mnemonics.title: Flip-Flop Types
mnemonics.content: R-S-T-D-J-K: Remember behavior - RS: Reset-Set, T: Toggle, D: Data/Delay, JK: Jack-Kilby (most versatile).

mnemonics.title: Multiplexer Selection
mnemonics.content: 2^n inputs = n select lines. Think: Select lines INDEX the input you want.

mnemonics.title: Decoder Logic
mnemonics.content: n inputs → 2^n outputs. Only ONE output HIGH at a time. Think: DECode = Decipher which line is active.

acronyms.expansion: Multiplexer - Many inputs to One output
acronyms.usage: Used in data selection, routing signals

acronyms.expansion: Demultiplexer - One input to Many outputs
acronyms.usage: Used in data distribution, address decoding
Standard digital logic family, uses transistors

visualTips.visualization: Draw as inequality symbol: A ≠ B. Output 1 when inputs DIFFER.
Think of it as odd number detector - output 1 when odd number of 1s (1 or 3).
Draw state as ball on hill - unstable at top (metastable), rolls to 0 or 1 stable states.
Draw as waterfall - each flip-flop triggers next, creating ripple effect.
Think of it as rotary switch - select line chooses which input connects to output.

analogies.analogy: A calculator: Press keys (inputs), immediately see result (output) - no memory of previous calculations.
A combination lock: Current position (state) + new input (dial turn) = new state. Previous state matters!
A light switch - toggles between ON and OFF states, stays in that state until next trigger.
Heartbeat of CPU - regular pulses that synchronize all operations, like metronome for musicians.
Water flowing through pipes - time taken for water (signal) to travel from input to output end.
Latch is like a door that stays open/closed based on handle position; Flip-Flop is like door that only responds to specific handle movements (clock edges).
